headline: FOURTH MAJOR HIT: Americas Gold Finds 1,392 g/t Silver in Six New Idaho Galena Veins
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) announced its fourth major new discovery at the Idaho Galena Complex today — Q1 2026 drilling defined six new high-grade silver-copper-antimony veins including 1,392 g/t Ag, 1.5% Cu, and 1.5% Sb over 1.9 metres. The 43L-TJ Vein sits just 25 metres from existing underground infrastructure, open for expansion.

headline: TECK BACKS KODIAK: Major Miner Partners With Junior to Spin Out US Copper Explorer in Arizona
snippet: Kodiak Copper (TSXV: KDK) and Teck Resources signed a non-binding LOI to merge Kodiak's Mohave property and Teck's Copper Hill property — both Arizona porphyry copper targets — into a new TSXV-listed US-focused exploration company. The combined 52-sq.-km land package sits in an established porphyry district; closing is targeted for Q3 2026.

headline: NEVADA KING DOUBLES DRILL PROGRAM TO 40,000M at Atlanta; BLM Clears 33 New Sites After 20.8 g/t Surface Hit
snippet: Nevada King Gold (TSXV: NKG) doubled its Phase 4 Atlanta gold project drill program to 40,000 metres of reverse circulation and reported new Silver Park assay results, including 0.62 g/t Au over 13.7 metres. The Bureau of Land Management approved 33 new drill sites at Western Rim — the area where a surface chip sample returned 20.8 g/t gold, the project's all-time record.

headline: 6 RIGS TURNING UNDERGROUND: FireFly Metals Green Bay High-Grade Core Drives Imminent PEA
snippet: FireFly Metals (ASX/TSX: FFM) reported outstanding drilling from the high-grade Core Zone at Green Bay, Newfoundland, with assays confirming grade and continuity needed to anchor early project economics. Six rigs are active underground, with a mineral resource estimate update feeding directly into a preliminary economic assessment.

headline: WORLD BANK CAUTION: Gold Near $4,500 Has Hit a Ceiling — Rally Runs on Geopolitics, Not Structure
snippet: The World Bank's April 2026 Commodity Markets Outlook forecasts a modest 5% rise in precious metals for the year but warns that gold — after surging 41% in 2025 — is near the high end of valuation models. The bank says further upside depends on fresh geopolitical shocks, not structural demand, and investors should expect elevated volatility rather than sustained trend gains.

headline: VISIBLE GOLD AT 190M DEPTH: Emperor Metals Pushes Duquesne West Strike 1.5km East in Quebec
snippet: Emperor Metals (CSE: AUAU) intersected visible gold in drill-hole DQ26-06 at 190 metres depth — 1.5 kilometres east of the conceptual open-pit at Duquesne West, Quebec. The project holds an inferred resource of 1.46Moz at 1.69 g/t Au, with a reference intercept of 21.7m at 35.2 g/t suggesting the east extension could materially expand the resource.

headline: EVERYONE AGREES IT'S A CRISIS — Yet Gold Is Slipping and Mining Stocks Are Still Lagging the Metal
snippet: Despite wall-to-wall crisis narratives and gold trading above $4,500/oz, a contrarian analysis argues that mining equities continue to significantly underperform the metal itself. The piece examines persistent cost inflation, permitting delays, and retail sentiment cycles as structural reasons why gold producers haven't captured the value the spot price implies they should.

headline: CHINA SILVER DEMAND UNSTOPPABLE: Record 790+ Tonnes Imported in Early 2026 on Solar, Retail Surge
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly Gold SWOT analysis flags China's all-time record silver imports in March 2026, driven by the solar industry and retail investors front-running anticipated trade restrictions. The six-year structural supply deficit — projected at 67 million ounces for 2026 — means drawdowns from above-ground inventories are accelerating with no demand softening in sight.

headline: RARE EARTH POWER PLAY: USA Rare Earth's $2.8B Serra Verde Deal Locks Up All 4 Magnetic Metals Outside Asia
snippet: USA Rare Earth (USAR) is up 68% year-to-date after announcing a $2.8 billion acquisition of Brazil's Serra Verde — described as the only producer outside Asia capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths at scale, targeting $550M–$650M EBITDA by end-2027. Motley Fool's analysis argues the deal is strategically timed as US policy accelerates supply chain separation from China.

headline: DEAL FEVER: Mining M&A Hits $21.6 Billion in Q1 2026 — Strongest First Quarter Since 2023
snippet: Global mining M&A reached $21.6 billion across 121 transactions in Q1 2026 — a 34% jump from Q1 2025 and the strongest sector start since 2023, per White & Case data. Gold leads consolidation targets as producers near record margins, with 32% of surveyed executives expecting strategic joint ventures to be the dominant deal structure for the rest of the year.
